content.full_text:
July 2, 1990

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street Room 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

On July 2, 1990, I spoke with Susan Bobrow, Esq., of your office regarding the applicability of Proposition 73 contribution limitations. In particular, I asked her for advice regarding applicable restrictions on contributions made by partnerships and their controlling partners.

Ms. Bobrow discussed this matter with several persons, including technical assistance, and advised me that if two partners, each having a 50% ownership in a partnership, currently decide to make a partnership contribution of $1,000 in a fiscal year to a candidate, then the individual partners may still each give $1,000 to the same candidate. Also, if one partnership were to give $1,000, then other partnerships controlled by the same partners would not be able to make any contributions. In other words, all of the partnerships affiliated with and controlled by two partners would be subject to a single cumulative $1,000 limit.

I am aware of the recent staff letter to Lance Olson, dated May 11, 1990, FPPC File Number A-90-302. In that advice letter, your office determined that an elected officer could accept contributions from two general partners of a business entity, even though the business entity had already made a $1,000 contribution to the campaign. The rationale was that certain partners had made the decision to contribute, and neither of the other two partners directed or controlled the contribution made by the entity.

This advice seems inapposite to the advice communicated from technical assistance through Ms. Bobrow. I would like to receive written confirmation of advice applying to my client's circumstances. Simply put, there are two general partners each owning 50%, who control 10 to 15 different general partnerships. Sometimes the individual partnerships might make contributions and sometimes the individual partners might make

Scott Hallabrin  
July 2, 1990  
Page Two

contributions. The two individual partners control and direct each of the partnerships, but do not control or direct each other. I would like to receive advice as to the application of contribution limits under different factual variations whereby (a) the individual partners make contributions to the same candidate but none of the partnerships do, (b) one or more of the partnerships make contributions to the same candidate, and (c) one or both of the individual partners and one or more of the partnerships make contributions to a particular candidate.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. Please call me if you have any questions.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Peter A. Bagatelos
